title: Pterostylis alobula
taxonomy: Pterostylis alobula is classified as a species within the genus Pterostylis, which is a group of orchids commonly known as the 'greenhood orchids'. This classification is based on the plant's unique characteristics, such as its green or yellowish-green flowers and its habit of growing in terrestrial environments. The genus Pterostylis is a part of the Orchidaceae family, which comprises over 30,000 known species of orchids worldwide. Pterostylis alobula is native to the island of Tasmania in Australia, where it grows in woodland and grassland ecosystems. The species is endemic to this region, meaning it is found nowhere else in the wild.

intro: Pterostylis alobula, also known as the greenhood orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to Australia.
